message:
fix(tokio-compat): drive Tokio futures on actual runtime and apply const fn promotions

The tokio-compat layer previously only entered the Tokio handle context
(Handle::enter) without actually driving the Tokio runtime. Futures that
used tokio::spawn, Tokio timers, or other driver-backed facilities would
hang because no one was polling the Tokio reactor.

runtime.rs:
- Rewrite with_tokio_context() to run the user closure on an Asupersync
  blocking thread via block_on_sync(), calling tokio::Runtime::block_on()
  inside. This means the Tokio current-thread runtime actually drives
  the future, supporting tokio::spawn, timers, and I/O.
- Update function signature: F and T now require Send + 'static bounds
  since the closure crosses a thread boundary.
- Handle panics from the blocking pool by re-panicking with the message.
- Update docs to reflect that Tokio is the actual executor for wrapped
  futures, not just a context provider.
- Replace runtime_is_reused_on_same_thread test with a simpler Arc
  pointer comparison via with_tokio_context_sync.
- Add tokio_spawn_runs_inside_context test that verifies tokio::spawn
  actually completes inside with_tokio_context.

lib.rs:
- Update hard boundary rule #2 to clarify that Tokio may be used as a
  private executor on blocking threads for Tokio-only futures.
- Promote AdapterConfig builder methods to const fn.

blocking.rs:
- Promote BlockingOutcome::is_ok/is_cancelled/is_panicked to const fn.
- Refactor panic_message() from if-else chain to map_or_else pattern.
- Remove unused AtomicBool import and unused main_thread variable.
- Fix block_on test helper: remove unnecessary mut on parameter (pin!
  handles the pinning).

cancel.rs:
- Promote CancelAware::new() to const fn.

io.rs:
- Promote TokioIo and AsupersyncIo new/inner/inner_mut to const fn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
